title: hieracium auriculiforme subsp. subcallosum
intro: Hieracium auriculiforme subsp. subcallosum, also known as the hairy hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Hieracium auriculiforme subsp. subcallosum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, but caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
